2604.19566.title: Diagnosable ColBERT: Debugging Late-Interaction Retrieval Models Using a Learned Latent Space as Reference
2604.19566.paper_summary: Reliable biomedical and clinical retrieval requires more than strong ranking performance: it requires a practical way to find systematic model failures and curate the training evidence needed to correct them. Late-interaction models such as ColBERT provide a first solution thanks to the interpretable token-level interaction scores they expose between document and query tokens. Yet this interpretability is shallow: it explains a particular document--query pairwise score, but does not reveal whether the model has learned a clinical concept in a stable, reusable, and context-sensitive way across diverse expressions. As a result, these scores provide limited support for diagnosing misunderstandings, identifying irreasonably distant biomedical concepts, or deciding what additional data or feedback is needed to address this. In this short position paper, we propose Diagnosable ColBERT, a framework that aligns ColBERT token embeddings to a reference latent space grounded in clinical knowledge and expert-provided conceptual similarity constraints. This alignment turns document encodings into inspectable evidence of what the model appears to understand, enabling more direct error diagnosis and more principled data curation without relying on large batteries of diagnostic queries.
2604.19566.paper_summary_zh: 可靠的生物醫學和臨床檢索不僅需要強大的排名性能：還需要一種實用的方法來發現系統模型的失敗，並整理所需的訓練證據以進行修正。像 ColBERT 這樣的晚期互動模型提供了一個初步解決方案，因為它們揭示了文檔和查詢標記之間的可解釋的標記級互動分數。然而，這種可解釋性是淺層的：它解釋了特定文檔與查詢的成對分數，但並未揭示模型是否以穩定、可重用且具上下文敏感的方式學習了臨床概念。因此，這些分數對於診斷誤解、識別不合理的生物醫學概念距離，或決定需要什麼額外數據或反饋來解決這個問題的支持有限。在這篇簡短的立場論文中，我們提出了可診斷的 ColBERT，一個將 ColBERT 標記嵌入對齊到基於臨床知識和專家提供的概念相似性約束的參考潛在空間的框架。這種對齊將文檔編碼轉變為可檢查的證據，顯示模型似乎理解的內容，使得更直接的錯誤診斷和更有原則的數據整理成為可能，而不需要依賴大量的診斷查詢。
2604.19566.author: François Remy et.al.
